data.title: European markets end lower, hot U.S. jobs report weighs
data.description:
Update: The post has been updated to reflect market movement as of 11.45am ET.

London (UKX) -0.69%

Germany (DAX:IND) -0.16%

France (CAC:IND) -0.47%

Industrial production in France rose by 0.2% month-on-month in November.

Retail sales in Italy dropped by 0.4% month-over-month in November 2024, a smaller decline than the 0.5% drop in the previous month, but contrary to market expectations of a 0.2% increase.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) closed 0.83% lower on Friday, though it posted a second consecutive weekly gain. The index followed its American counterparts lower after a stronger-than-expected US jobs report reinforced the Fed's cautious approach to further rate cuts.

Concerns about inflation, the UK's debt crisis, and uncertainty over U.S. President-elect Donald Trump's tariff policies also weighed on investor sentiment.

Meanwhile, the British pound slumped to its lowest against the U.S. dollar in more than a year, trading at $1.2284 earlier in the session.

On the corporate front, Ubisoft (OTCPK:UBSFY) shares fell after the French video game maker said it had appointed strategic advisors to review its options following reports last year that a buyout was under consideration.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up 6 basis points to 4.75%.

Germany's 10-year yield was almost unchanged at 2.57%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 3 basis points to 4.84%.

In the upcoming week, investors will look forward to UK consumer price index (CPI), producer price index (PPI) and retail sales figures for December, along with data on the region's industrial and manufacturing production in November. German and French CPI for the month of December will also be on the radar. For the broader Eurozone, December CPI and November industrial production figures are due next week.

Meanwhile, the world's largest economy, U.S., is set to report December PPI, CPI, industrial and manufacturing production in the next week.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (EWG), (GF), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM), (DAX), (FLGR), (FXB), (EWU), (FKU), (EWUS), (FLGB), (GREK)

Seeking Alpha Editor Khushi Singh contributed to this article

More on markets

  • Trump tariff fears causing disconnect in silver, copper markets - Bloomberg
  • Asian markets continue downward trend over Fed policy concerns
  • Euro stocks can offer a lesson in high concentration for the U.S. – Goldman

data.title: Arthur J. Gallagher acquires Wealth Management Partners
data.description:
• Arthur J. Gallagher & Co. (NYSE:AJG) on Friday announced the acquisition of Wealth Management Partners.
  • Terms of the transaction were not disclosed.
  • Wealth Management Partners is a financial planning firm specializing in retirement plan risk management for individuals and corporate clients in Western Australia, said the company.
  • AJG is -0.15% to $285.06.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Precision Bio rises after gene editing data: BMO upgrades (update)
data.description:
Update 11.29 AM EST: Adds details on BMO’s upgrade and current stock price

Precision BioSciences (NASDAQ:DTIL) added ~26% in the morning hours on Friday after the gene-editing company said ECUR-506, one of its lead candidates, generated a complete clinical response in a Phase 1/2 trial for Ornithine Transcarbamylase (OTC) deficiency, a rare metabolic disorder.

A therapeutic option that can address the accumulation of blood ammonia, a characteristic of OTC deficiency, is critical to avoid disease outcomes such as neurological damage, coma, and death.

According to data from DTIL's OTC-HOPE study, the first male infant treated with ECUR-506 had a normal mean ammonia level among other responses following a single dose of Precision Bio's (NASDAQ:DTIL) experimental therapy.

The company said the patient managed to maintain the response over six months, indicating a complete response. As for safety, ECUR-506 was generally well tolerated without significant tolerability concerns in the patient except for asymptomatic transaminitis, according to Precision Bio (NASDAQ:DTIL).

OTC-HOPE, the first-in-human trial for ECUR-506, is currently underway in countries including the U.S. and the U.K. DTIL's partner iECURE intends to finish its enrollments this year and disclose complete data in H1 2026.

With a $34 price target, BMO Capital Markets analyst Kostas Biliouris upgraded the North Carolina-based biotech to Outperform from Market Perform noting an attractive valuation following the readout, which he said seemed "very promising."

The analyst cites a favorable risk-reward setup and argues that the data provides early clinical evidence to validate Precision's (DTIL) ARCUS gene editing platform, while the company trades at roughly one-third of its cash level.

data.title: Reaction Roundup: SA analysts, experts weigh in on December jobs report
data.description:
Wall Street's focus on Friday was on the December 2024 jobs report. Employment growth jumped in the final month of last year, helped by uptrends in health care, government, and social assistance. Meanwhile, jobs in retail trade swung to a gain in December from a loss in November.

According to the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS), total nonfarm payrolls increased by 256K in December, significantly outpacing the consensus of +157K. The unemployment rate ticked down to 4.1%, coming in lower than the expected figure of 4.2%.

U.S. stocks (SP500) slumped while bonds sold off after the data, as traders further recalibrated their Federal Reserve rate cut odds following the clear indication of a resilient labor market. Here are some exchange-traded funds that track the benchmark index: (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), and (SPXU).

See below for various reactions to the jobs report:

Michael Kramer, founder of Mott Capital Management and investing group leader of Reading The Markets:

"The job report for December came in very strong, with the unemployment rate falling as fewer workers lost jobs and the number of entrants into the labor force declined. The index of aggregate weekly payrolls suggests the economy is growing at a nominal rate of around 5%, indicating some reacceleration since early summer.

The strong headline numbers and lower unemployment increase the odds that the Fed has made a policy error, suggesting the rate-cutting cycle is probably over. This will make next week’s CPI report even more important."

Victor Dergunov, investing group leader of The Financial Prophet:

"223K private sector jobs could take the March rate cut off the table. It will certainly reduce the probability of a March rate cut substantially. The good news is that the report did not come in significantly worse than expected. This dynamic illustrates that the U.S. economy remains highly resilient.

Nonetheless, I am concerned about higher rates and the dollar pressuring equities here in the near term. Also, the market could shake it off here, which could enable a rally again soon."

Sarah House, senior economist at Wells Fargo:

"The FOMC made clear at its December meeting that it would take additional progress on moving inflation back to 2% before future rate cuts materialized, assuming that the labor market remained in a healthy position. Today's employment data should give policymakers more confidence in that assumption. A solid pace of hiring as measured by the nonfarm payrolls figures, when paired with an unemployment rate that has been relatively flat since the summer, likely will push the central bank to be patient with future rate cuts.

We still expect the FOMC to cut rates again at some point this year as it searches for the neutral rate, but a rate cut at its meeting on January 29 is off the table, and March looks increasingly unlikely as well."

Council of Economic Advisers, The White House:

"Today’s jobs report for December shows that the labor market remains in good shape. Payrolls were up 256,000, well ahead of expectations, and the unemployment rate ticked down to 4.1%.

Employment grew substantially in private education and health services, leisure and hospitality, and retail trade, but fell slightly in manufacturing.

On average during 2024, employment grew 186,000, lower than in recent years but above the 2019 average. In only 16% of the years since 1948 has the jobless rate been lower than 2024’s 4.0%. Persistently low unemployment rates help to support worker bargaining clout."

Jason Furman, former deputy director of the U.S. National Economic Council:

"A strong jobs report. 256,000 jobs added. Unemployment rate ticks down to 4.1%. Participation rate and hours stable while prime age employment up. And slow nominal wage growth will be reassuring to the Fed. Some of this may be bounce back from strikes, hurricanes, etc.

Bottom line: (really the) economy remains very robust. A variety of indicators show the labor market has stabilized. There doesn't seem to be any pressing reason to change interest rates any time soon."

Alberto Gallo, chief investment officer at Andromeda Capital Management:

"The Fed's policy mistake is becoming more evident. Intra-election softness wasn't an emergency, and today the U.S. labor market is back to full speed.

Going ahead, supply bottlenecks and fiscal dominance will make inflation stickier. A U-turn in policy is coming."

Renaissance Macro Research:

"Strong employment report. But, one area of concern will be the real estate industry. Real estate employment is falling against last year. With rates continuing to jump, there is some risk that residential construction employment follows."

data.title: BioCryst releases preliminary 2024 results, 2025 guidance
data.description:
BioCryst (NASDAQ:BCRX) released its preliminary 2024 revenue results, along with guidance for 2025.

The company said it expects to report 2024 net revenue of $450M, up 36% from the prior year, with net revenue from its drug Orladeyo rising 34% to $437M. For Q4 2024, net revenue shot up 40% to $130.8M, including $123.5M from Orladeyo.

For 2025, BioCryst (NASDAQ:BCRX) anticipates reporting total revenue of $540M to $560M, with Orladeyo revenue in the range of $515M to $535M. The company expects to approach quarterly EPS profitability/positive cash flow in the second half of 2025, according to a statement.

Analysts, on average, were expecting the company to report Q4 revenue of $128.65M, 2024 revenue of $446.22M and 2025 revenue of $533.76M.

The company also expects to see an annual revenue CAGR of around 20% over the next three years, with an annual operating expense CAGR of around 5%.

BioCryst (BCRX) also plans to submit a new drug application in 2025 for Orladeyo granules for the treatment of children up to age 12.

data.title: Edison extends losses as insurance firms seek to preserve California wildfire evidence
data.description:
Edison International (NYSE:EIX) -5.4% in Friday's trading to its lowest in nearly a year after its SoCal Edison power utility was asked by attorneys representing insurance companies to preserve evidence in connection with the Eaton fire, one of several blazes that erupted in the Los Angeles area this week during extreme winds.

In a filing posted late Thursday with California regulators, SoCal Edison (NYSE:EIX) said the filing was triggered by online publications that "seemingly suggest" the utility's equipment may have been associated with the fire's ignition, but "to date, no fire agency has suggested that SCE's electric facilities were involved in the ignition or requested the removal and retention of any SCE equipment."

The utility said in the filing that its preliminary analysis showed transmission lines near the start of the blaze were energized, and showed no interruptions or electrical or operational anomalies until an hour after the reported start time of the wildfire.

SoCal Edison (EIX) said the reported area of origin of the Palisades fire is not in its service area, but it does have some infrastructure around the sites of the Hurst and Eaton fires and is reviewing the incidents.

Edison's (EIX) after-tax liability cap would total ~$3.2B, roughly the same as Wednesday's decline in equity value, Jefferies analyst Paul Zimbardo said, and that he believes "the market is overestimating the exposure," which would explain Wednesday's 10% stock drop; he rates the shares as a Buy with a $93 price target.

Even if utility infrastructure was found to not be involved in the latest fires, utility stocks likely will trade at a wider discount than before, Jefferies analysts said, adding that if "the number of structures and damage estimates gets unwieldy high approaching the roughly $15B current fund size, investors will likely get nervous on incremental exposure."

data.title: 4D Molecular, FDA agree on phase 3 design for DME candidate
data.description:
4D Molecular Therapeutics (NASDAQ:FDMT) and the U.S. FDA have reached agreement on a phase 3 trial design for diabetic macular edema (DME) candidate 4D-150.

The company will only have to conduct a single pivotal trial due to data from 4D-150 in wet age-related macular degeneration and a planned phase 3 program in this other indication.

About 300-400 patients will be enrolled with a primary endpoint of best corrected visual acuity (BCVA) noninferiority vs. Regeneron Pharmaceuticals' (REGN) Eylea (aflibercept). The 4D-150 dose chosen for phase 3 is 3E10 vg/eye.

4D Molecular also released interim data from the phase 2 SPECTRA trial. Through 32 weeks, those in the 3E10 vg/eye group given a single dose saw a sustained gain in BCVA of +8.4 letters and a reduction in central subfield thickness of -194 µm.

Also, those in the 3E10 vg/eye cohort experienced a reduction in supplemental injections of of 86% vs. projected Eylea 2mg once every 8 weeks dosing.

Separately, 4D Molecular said that it would focus its pipeline on 4D-150 for wet AMD and DME and 4D-710 for cystic fibrosis. As a result, its expects to have enough cash on hand to fund operations into 2028.

4D Molecular is down ~6% in Friday morning trading.

data.title: Smartsheet ticks higher after EU approves sale to Blackstone,Vista Equity
data.description:
• Smartsheet (NYSE:SMAR) edged higher by 0.3% after the European Union approved the company's $8.4 billion sale to private equity firms Blackstone (BX) and Vista Equity.
  • The European antitrust regulator approved the deal, according to a statement on the European Commission's website.
  • "The Commission concluded that the notified transaction would not raise competition concerns, given the companies' limited combined market position resulting from the proposed transaction," the EC said in the statement.
  • Smartsheet (NYSE:SMAR) announced on Sept. 24 a deal to sell itself to private equity firms Vista Equity and Blackstone (BX) for $56.50 a share.

data.title: Coinbase said to face CFTC subpoena for Polymarket customer data
data.description:
Coinbase Global (NASDAQ:COIN) has been subpoenaed by the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission to provide data on customers interacting with prediction market firm Polymarket, according to a media report dated Friday.

The cryptocurrency exchange notified its customers saying it may have to share that data with the derivatives regulator, CoinDesk reported, citing a person familiar with the matter.

The news comes as the CFTC has engaged in an extended legal conflict more broadly with prediction market companies, with the latest action occurring mere days before a transition to Republican from Democratic leadership at the CFTC, as President-elect Donald Trump prepares to take office.

"When we receive requests for information from a government, each request is carefully reviewed by a team of trained experts using established procedures to determine its legal sufficiency," a Coinbase spokesperson told CoinDesk in an emailed statement. Where necessary, we will seek to narrow requests that are overly broad or vague in order to provide a more appropriately tailored response, and in some cases we object to producing any information at all."

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Intra-Cellular settles Caplyta patent dispute with Sandoz
data.description:
• Shares of Intra-Cellular Therapies (NASDAQ:ITCI) climbed on Friday after the neurology drugmaker settled a patent dispute with generic drug developer Sandoz (OTCQX:SDZNY) over its antipsychotic therapy Caplyta (lumateperone).
  • Sandoz’s (OTCQX:SDZXF) submission of an abbreviated new drug application with the FDA to market a generic version of the drug in the U.S. led to the litigation filed in a New Jersey District Court.
  • As part of the settlement, Intra-Cellular (NASDAQ:ITCI) has allowed the Swiss drugmaker to market a generic version of Caplyta from July 1, 2040, or earlier, subject to certain conditions.
  • The agreement is expected to take effect following approvals from the U.S. Federal Trade Commission and the U.S. Department of Justice. ITCI’s lawsuits against other parties over Caplyta patents remain unresolved at the New Jersey District Court.
